summary:
Given an array of integers and a number k, return the k values that occur most often in the array.

The answer may be returned in any order, and the test is guaranteed to have a unique set of top-k elements.

examples.explanation: 1 appears three times and 2 appears twice, making them the two most frequent values.
6 appears four times and 4 appears three times, so they are the two most frequent.

constraints: 1 <= k <= number of distinct values in nums
The set of top-k frequent elements is unique
